title: stop auto-banning users with deleted github accounts
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Put down the hammer
gist.blurb: Our automated tier-progression bot was getting a little too enthusiastic and banning users with suspended GitHub accounts. We've taken away its ban-hammer, so your accounts are safe from friendly fire.
gist.summary: Disabled the automated cron job that incorrectly banned users with suspended GitHub accounts during tier progression.
gist.impact: Users with temporarily suspended GitHub accounts will no longer face automatic platform bans.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about stopping an automatic ban process. Visually, this can be represented by Polly the bee taking a giant 'ban hammer' away from the eager Monitor Robot, ensuring safety for the users (represented by seeds/spores).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny hardhat, carefully taking a giant pixelated hammer away from a chunky monitor-headed robot. The robot's screen shows a relieved smiley face. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny hardhat, carefully taking a giant pixelated hammer away from a chunky monitor-headed robot.
